Keep first-party routing and virtual scrolling aligned with real browsers

This commit finalizes two isolated runtime fixes left in the working tree.
The proxy now recognizes same-origin requests by comparing the browser's
Origin against the Host header, which keeps first-party API access working
when Next.js dev normalizes origins oddly on LAN or local hostnames. The
shared virtual list also mirrors its self-scroll element into a ref at
commit time so TanStack Virtual subscribes reliably instead of rendering 0
items when React callback refs schedule state too late.

Constraint: Browser-origin checks must stay strict for cross-origin traffic while still allowing real first-party requests in local and LAN development
Constraint: TanStack Virtual subscribes to the scroll element synchronously during mount, so callback-ref state alone can miss the first subscription window
Rejected: Loosen proxy origin checks globally | would weaken same-origin guarantees for protected API routes
Rejected: Keep virtual list on state-only callback refs | intermittent empty virtualization state remains possible on first mount
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Preserve the Host-header fallback and commit-time scroll ref mirror unless a replacement is verified against local, LAN, and restricted-browser contexts
Tested: pnpm test tests/list-virtualization.test.ts tests/salesforce-feed-auth.test.ts
Not-tested: Full manual QA across every first-party route that depends on proxy same-origin detection
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Keep automation and scroll helpers from fighting runtime reality

This commit hardens the remaining operational paths that were still open
in the working tree: authenticated scrape triggers can bypass the public
IP rate limiter, the shared virtual list now holds its self-scroll node in
state, Flextender drops a stray form field, and the embedding batch now
uses the final embedding text as its eligibility gate. It also teaches the
scraper health task to force-reset platforms that have been stuck behind a
circuit breaker for 48 hours and routes Trigger Sentry init through the
same safe DSN validation used elsewhere.

Constraint: Automation endpoints need to stay usable for cron and internal callers without weakening the public rate limit path
Constraint: Embedding backfills must remain cheap and deterministic on the visible vacature set
Rejected: Keep relying on raw sourceText checks before embedding | duplicated logic and diverged from the actual text sent to embeddings
Rejected: Leave stale scraper circuit breakers untouched indefinitely | keeps known-bad platforms wedged without recovery pressure
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: moderate
Reversibility: clean
Directive: If scrape-start auth changes again, preserve the split between bearer-authorized automation and public IP-throttled access
Tested: pnpm test tests/embeddings-batch.test.ts tests/list-virtualization.test.ts tests/flextender-hours.test.ts tests/trigger-config-env-sync.test.ts
Not-tested: End-to-end browser interaction for the scrape start button and long-running stale breaker recovery in production time

commit.message:
Reduce skills complexity and keep scraper + Sentry stable in runtime

This completes the current LinkedIn-style skills cleanup by routing the
remaining app, AI, MCP, and voice surfaces through the simplified
canonical skill model and removing the root app dependency on the
workspace ESCO package. It also hardens scraper runtime behavior with
short-lived caches and request-level fallbacks so the dashboard keeps
rendering during Neon timeouts, and it adds defensive Sentry DSN
validation so malformed DSNs no longer produce browser console noise.

Constraint: The app still needs compatibility aliases because several public and internal surfaces still speak in canonicalSkills and legacy ESCO-shaped payloads during migration
Constraint: Scraper dashboard reliability had to improve without rewriting the entire data model or depending on a stable external database during dev
Rejected: Full removal of the esco facade and payload field names in one pass | too risky while downstream consumers still expect compatibility
Rejected: Let invalid Sentry DSNs flow into SDK init and rely on runtime warnings | noisy and user-visible in development
Confidence: medium
Scope-risk: broad
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep the compatibility shims until the remaining escoUri/ESCO naming debt and legacy endpoints are intentionally retired in a follow-up cleanup
Tested: pnpm lint; pnpm exec tsc --noEmit; pnpm test; zsh -lc 'set -a; source /tmp/motian.env; set +a; pnpm build'; runtime checks for /, /kandidaten, /vacatures, /chat, /scraper, /api/gezondheid on dev server
Not-tested: Authenticated /api/vaardigheden behavior and correcting the underlying Sentry DSN value in env storage
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(catchup): purge hallucinated entries from history.json

Three entries introduced during the 2026-04-10 to 2026-04-13 fallback
period have been verified as hallucinations and removed:

- "Introducing Claude Managed Agents" (anthropic.com/news/claude-managed-agents)
  HTTP 404 — URL does not exist on Anthropic's site.
- "Claude Cowork is now generally available" (anthropic.com/news/claude-cowork-ga)
  HTTP 404 — URL does not exist.
- "Introducing upgrades to Codex" (openai.com/index/introducing-upgrades-to-codex/)
  Dated 2026-04-11 but absent from the live OpenAI RSS top-30 despite being
  recent enough to appear; very likely fabricated by WebSearch fallback.

These entries slipped in while trigger-side WebFetch was 403-blocked and the
prompt was allowed to synthesize from WebSearch snippets. The new architecture
(local fetcher → cache JSON → trigger reads cache, no fallback) prevents this
class of error going forward. Prior daily/weekly report files are left as
historical record; future reports will no longer reference these entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs/installer/*.md instead of the LLM-invented approximations that

reading as stuck to users pressing Enter on a spinning bar.

Intro flow (new):
- Before the phase loop, render 0_welcome_step.zsh.md,
  1_Explain_step.zsh.md and 2_listing.zsh.md verbatim — banner,
  content, footer hint, nav bar and FRAMEWRK tag all preserved. The
  nav bar is the consistent reference shared with the advanced
  interactive flow reached via `make setup-dev`
  (`vetcoders_install.py install --advanced`), not a per-screen
  promise, so we no longer strip it.
- The intro flow supports ENTER=proceed, b/back=step back, q=quit.
  Back only applies inside the intro screens — once a phase has
  committed side effects there is nothing safe to un-do.
- Intro owns its own banner, so `_print_title` is skipped when the
  flow runs. Dry-run and auto_yes paths still get the compact title.

Phase consent:
- Pause Rich Progress Live around each consent call — a spinning bar
  next to a still prompt read as "working", not "waiting for you",
  and Rich was truncating the `cur` column on narrow terminals,
  producing "awaiting consen" + a ghost spinner frame.
- The consent prompt now renders as a yellow rule with an explicit
  "ENTER=run   n/q=cancel" hint line. The sticky bar only appears
  while a phase is actually running, so subprocess output scrolls
  above it cargo-style.
- Remove the "(optional phase)" / "(optional)" labels from the
  consent prompt and reason block header, and drop `s=skip` from the
  accepted responses. The internal `optional` flag still governs
  whether a phase failure is fatal or just a warning, but it no
  longer leaks into user-facing copy.

  Verification now match the `2_listing.zsh.md` mock literally,

- Drop the apologetic "Read-only. We touch nothing on your system
  during this step." and "Read-only. We run `vibecrafted doctor`..."

Landing summary:
- Rule-bordered verdict ("⚒ Vibecrafted. is ready" on success,
  stopped/warnings on failure), followed by a "Next steps" block
  with concrete commands (`vibecrafted doctor`, `vc-start`,
  `vibecrafted --help`, `vibecrafted update`) and a docs link. Users
  landing on 3–4 screens of scrollback now get an unmistakable
  landing page instead of a bare `Log: …` line.

- `uv run vetcoders-installer install.toml --dry-run` renders cleanly.

* fix(docs/installer): normalize FRAMEWORK tag to consistent squared block

The FRAMEWORK figlet tag at the bottom of every mock screen mixed three
different Unicode blocks, which made some glyphs render differently —
or not at all — depending on the terminal font:

  -  🅵  U+1F175  NEGATIVE SQUARED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER F
  -  🅁  U+1F141  SQUARED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER R
  -  🄰  U+1F130  SQUARED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER A
  -  🄼  U+1F13C  SQUARED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER M
  -  🄴  U+1F134  SQUARED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER E
  -  🅆  U+1F146  SQUARED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER W
  -  🄞  U+1F11E  PARENTHESIZED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER O   ← wrong block
  -  🅁  U+1F141  SQUARED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER R
  -  🅺  U+1F17A  NEGATIVE SQUARED LATIN CAPITAL LETTER K

The parenthesized O and the negative-squared F/K broke visual rhythm
with the squared RAMEW/R in the middle — it read as "FRAMEW?RK" on
fonts that fell back for the O glyph. Replace every occurrence across
the mocks with the pure SQUARED block (U+1F130–U+1F149):

  🄵·🅁·🄰·🄼·🄴·🅆·🄾·🅁·🄺

All nine letters now come from the same Unicode block, so they all
render with the same weight and baseline on any font that carries the
enclosed-alphanumeric-supplement range.

Fixed in all seven files that carry the tag:
  - docs/installer/0_welcome_step.zsh.md
  - docs/installer/1_Explain_step.zsh.md
  - docs/installer/2_listing.zsh.md
  - docs/installer/3_examine.zsh.md
  - docs/installer/4_result.zsh.md
  - docs/installer/5_installation.zsh.md
  - docs/installer/DESIGN.md

Also fix a FRAMEWRK → FRAMEWORK typo in the installer docstring that
referenced the tag.

* fix(installer): truthful landing page — no fake URL, no fake commands

The cancelled-install path was promoting commands that assume the
install succeeded, plus a docs URL I invented wholesale:

  Before:
    ⚒ Install finished with warnings
    · Diagnostics  cancelled
    What now?
      ▸ vibecrafted doctor     audit the warnings above
      ▸ vc-start                launch the dashboard anyway
    Docs: https://vibecrafted.dev  ·  Updates: vibecrafted update

Three lies in five lines:

1. "Install finished with warnings" — there were no warnings. The
   user pressed q. We now headline pure-cancel runs with
   "⚒ Install cancelled — nothing changed" (yellow rule) and surface
   the cancelled phases explicitly before the next-step block.

2. "vibecrafted doctor / vc-start" — on a cancelled run the
   `vibecrafted` binary is not on PATH and there is no dashboard
   state to open. Recommending them is advertising features the
   user cannot reach. The pure-cancel branch now shows only one
   command: `make vibecrafted` to re-enter the installer when
   ready.

3. "https://vibecrafted.dev" — that domain does not exist. I made
   it up. The real public URL (sitemap + docs + the author's
   0_welcome_step mock) is https://vibecrafted.io. Footer now uses
   it, and we drop the "Updates: vibecrafted update" trailing line
   on every path because that command only exists after a
   successful install.

Also:
- Split the summary into four verdicts instead of three: all_ok,
  pure_cancel, has_fail, warnings. Previously has_cancel fell into
  the warnings branch which made the headline and next-step copy
  both wrong.
- Warnings path no longer suggests `vc-start` — we do not know how
  far the install actually got, so dashboard readiness is not
  promised. `vibecrafted doctor` stays (it reports on whatever got
  installed, which is legitimate here) plus `make vibecrafted`
  to re-run.
- Failed path drops the `vibecrafted doctor` suggestion for the
  same reason — the install bailed, we do not know if the doctor
  binary is even reachable — and just points at the log + re-run.

Verification:
- `python3 -m py_compile` on the edited module.
- PTY smoke test: enter intro flow 3x, quit at first consent,
  confirmed the new "Install cancelled — nothing changed" block
  prints with only `make vibecrafted` and `vibecrafted.io`.

Gemini 3.1 (gemini-3.1-pro-preview) emits reasoning in a thoughts[]

* installer: make TUI manifest-driven

- Moved hardcoded branding to [branding] in install.toml
- Moved hardcoded diagnostics to [diagnostics] in install.toml

- Replaced hardcoded text in markdown mockups dynamically using manifest values in _interpolate_mock

* vc-partner: add Whole-System Mandate

Bump skill version to 2.1.0 and add a Whole-System Mandate to SKILL.md. The new section expands the agent contract beyond the repo to include live system state (configs, runtime, infra, DBs, logs, deploy scripts, etc.) when accessible and necessary, while requiring actions be auditable, minimally sufficient, reversible, and aligned with the user's mandate. Also updates the allowed-initiative list to include inspecting runtime truth, checking env/config drift, and validating live logs/containers/health checks.

* Polish installer docs, branding, layout

Refine installer wording/layout and harden branding interpolation.

- Docs: tighten and clarify copy in welcome, explain, and listing steps; simplify navigation hints; fix tooling typo (zellij) and reformat checklist for readability.
- Branding: in vetcoders_installer/__init__.py strip trailing periods from manifest branding name and use a word-boundary regex replacement (\bVibecrafted\b) to avoid accidental substring replacements; removed the naive lowercase replace.

These changes improve clarity for users, prevent unintended text substitutions, and tweak UI spacing.

* Add worker contract to generated child plans

Append a Worker Contract section to the child plan template in spawn_marbles_write_child_plan (skills/vc-agents/scripts/lib/session.sh) that enforces hard rules for workers (stay on the assigned substrate, no branch/worktree/work relocation, return control if substrate invalid). Update tests (tests/tui/test_marbles_runtime.py) to read child plan contents into variables, keep existing header assertions, and add assertions that verify the new contract text is present in the generated child plans. Also refactors repeated file reads for clarity.

* Add 'delete' subcommand for marbles

Introduce a marbles `delete` control command to archive sessions under marbles/deleted/<run_id>. Implements _session_dir_for_run_id helper, cmd_delete (checks live watcher, moves session dir, and updates state.json with archived metadata), and integrates the new subcommand into command dispatch and help text. Adds a vetcoders wrapper function (marbles-delete) and tests covering successful archival and refusal for live sessions. Also updates launcher help output and unknown-command guidance to include `delete`.

* ﻿feat(platform): add shared desktop and mobile app heads

Summary:
- convert `PomodoroTimer/` into the shared Avalonia app layer and add
  `PomodoroTimer.Desktop/`, `PomodoroTimer.Android/`, and
  `PomodoroTimer.iOS/` entry-point projects
- introduce `PomodoroTimer.CrossPlatform.slnx`, responsive shared timer UI,
  and repo docs/guardrails that describe the new platform layout
- replace the placeholder desktop workflow with a real Windows/macOS
  build-and-test pipeline for the default solution

Rationale:
- the ticket requires explicit Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android support,
  which needs platform heads rather than a single desktop-only project
- keeping `PomodoroTimer.sln` focused on the shared app, desktop head, and
  tests preserves a stable .NET 8 validation path while mobile heads remain
  available for environments with the required .NET 10 mobile SDK/toolchains

commit.message:
fix(web): pin lodash-es for streamdown markdown (#698)

Summary:
- pin `lodash-es` to 4.18.1 through `pnpm.overrides` and refresh the web lockfile
- add a web CI check that fails if the security override regresses
- cover the markdown fallback path so mermaid fences stay non-rendering in app usage

Rationale:
- `MarkdownContent` uses `streamdown-svelte`, whose dependency graph still emits
  a `mermaid` chunk in the bundle graph and pulls in `lodash-es`
- the app does not enable the mermaid renderer today, but we still ship the
  dependency chain and should keep it on a patched version
- the fallback-path test documents current runtime reachability so reviewers can
  distinguish the shipped dependency from the disabled mermaid render path

Tests:
- `corepack pnpm run check:security-overrides`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/features/markdown/markdown-content.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `corepack pnpm exec prettier --check package.json scripts/check-security-overrides.mjs src/lib/features/markdown/markdown-content.test.ts`

feat: land mission control and production release flow (#33)

* refactor(cli): simplify version and mission control helpers

Extract shared helper reuse and tighten a few local control-flow paths
without changing behavior. This pulls git short SHA lookup into a shared
script helper, centralizes TUI session-id shortening, and keeps version
metadata resolution pure by separating build-override resolution from the
live git lookup gate.

The Mission Control command now uses small local snapshot-loading helpers
instead of repeating the same read/supervise wiring in multiple branches.
The modal renderer reuses a single fallback row rect instead of rebuilding
the same shape across branches, and the handoff redaction integration test
now asserts the redaction contract more directly.

Delete the temporary root-level TUI compatibility shims and point in-repo

imports now resolve from src/tui/state/*.

mission-control-commands.ts, updates the affected command/test imports, and

commit.message:
ref(cmdk) jsx powered cmdk (#112262)

Refactor CMDK to a composable JSX approach.

Previously, our CMDK required knowing actions aot and use a hook
registration process. We want to move to a fully JSX powered approach
and eventually also make actions render to the DOM (this is currently
not possible because virtualization is required and the listbox API
requires us to specify the full list)

This change brings us half way to being able to power the CMDK UI by
converting CMDK registration to JSX.

This enables some interesting patterns where the UI logic can be
colocated with the UI as seen
[here](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull/112262/changes/0158ce59c262d7ca3942ce2c913ddb8c09ae568b).

The way that this works is through the following mechanisms:
- Collection context responsible for storing nodes and edges
(essentially a recursive Context) that we use to maintain a
parent<->child mapping.
- A store with a `tree(root | undefined)` method which will reconstruct
the tree on-demand so that callers can have access to the most complete
UI at all times


The slots API mentioned above this are used in CMDK as contextual
priority slots that will appear as the first descendants of the CMDK
action (similar to how priority int value used to work before). CMDK now
has two slots, the first one is the `global` slot where global actions
should be registered, the second is the `page` where broader page
specific actions can be registered and the 3rd is the `task` slot, a
slot that is contextual to what the user is doing (e.g. if they are
editing a dashboard, saving or starring it might be a task they are
currently interested).

ASE-174: harden frontend i18n guardrails and CI gates (#676)

* feat(web): add frontend i18n guardrails

Summary:
- add a shared zh/en frontend i18n layer with persisted locale switching
  in the app shell user menu
- migrate key auth and layout surfaces to translated copy, including
  login, local bootstrap, navigation, top bar, and dashboard title
- add a configurable hardcoded-string scanner and wire it into web lint
  and CI, then document the new translation rules in the frontend README

Rationale:
- the app needed a single translation access path before broader copy
  migration could happen safely
- changed-lines i18n scanning gives CI a hard gate for new user-visible
  literals without blocking the existing legacy backlog all at once
- documenting exemptions and translation steps keeps the gradual rollout
  explicit for future contributors

Tests:
- cd web && pnpm run lint
- cd web && pnpm run check
- cd web && pnpm exec vitest run src/lib/i18n/index.test.ts src/lib/components/layout/top-bar.test.ts src/lib/components/layout/top-bar-user-menu.test.ts src/lib/components/layout/sidebar-nav.test.ts src/lib/components/layout/theme-root.test.ts
- cd web && pnpm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore(web): format layout theme tokens

Summary:
- format `web/src/routes/layout.css` with the repo Prettier version
  so the frontend CI gate matches local validation

Rationale:
- the shared frontend CI entrypoint runs `prettier --check .`, and this
  file failed that gate even though the feature work was otherwise ready

Tests:

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(web): handle shallow CI i18n diffs

Summary:
- make the i18n scanner fall back from `origin/main...HEAD` to a direct
  tree diff when shallow CI clones do not have a merge base available

Rationale:
- GitHub Actions checked out the PR head with limited history, which made
  the original three-dot diff fail even though the branch content was
  valid and already passed the equivalent local gate

Tests:
- cd web && pnpm run lint

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(web): expand i18n coverage and full scan gate

Summary:
- translate the tickets and agents pages, board filters, localized route
  titles, and priority labels for the newly added zh/en i18n layer
- switch the i18n scanner to full-tree, Svelte-section-aware scanning
  with a reviewed baseline ledger for existing legacy literals
- document the baseline workflow and add regression coverage for zh
  locale rendering on the affected surfaces

Rationale:
- review feedback showed the earlier diff-only scanner could not catch
  untranslated legacy pages that were still visible in the product
- a full-tree gate with an explicit baseline keeps CI blocking fresh
  regressions without forcing a one-shot migration of the whole app
- covering tickets and agents closes the most visible untranslated
  surfaces called out in rework feedback

Tests:
- export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" && pnpm --dir web run lint
- export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" && pnpm --dir web run check
- export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" && pnpm --dir web exec vitest run src/lib/features/board/priority.test.ts src/lib/features/board/components/priority-icon.test.ts src/lib/features/board/components/board-page.test.ts src/lib/features/agents/components/agents-page.test.ts
- export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" && PLAYWRIGHT_HOST=127.0.0.1 PLAYWRIGHT_PORT=50919 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:50919 PLAYWRIGHT_SERVER_MODE=preview pnpm --dir web run ci
- export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" && PLAYWRIGHT_HOST=127.0.0.1 PLAYWRIGHT_PORT=50919 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:50919 PLAYWRIGHT_SERVER_MODE=preview .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(web): fall back when rg is unavailable

Summary:
- make the i18n full-tree scan fall back to `git ls-files` when `rg`
  is not installed on the runner

Rationale:
- GitHub Actions failed the new scanner because the hosted runner did
  not expose `rg`, even though local development did
- keeping the fast `rg` path locally while falling back in CI preserves
  the stronger guardrail without depending on extra runner tooling

Tests:
- export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:/usr/bin:/bin" && cd web && node scripts/check-i18n.mjs
- export PATH="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH" && PLAYWRIGHT_HOST=127.0.0.1 PLAYWRIGHT_PORT=50919 PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:50919 PLAYWRIGHT_SERVER_MODE=preview .codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* 完善 i18n，完善 harness

* fix(web): harden i18n rollout quality gates

Summary:
- extract oversized web modules introduced by the i18n rollout so the
  file-budget and boundary checks stay green
- normalize locale copy and E2E selectors for Project AI, workflows,
  scheduled jobs, tickets, machines, activity, skills, and settings
- keep locale sorting and CI validation aligned so web lint, Playwright,
  and the repo CI gate all pass without suppressed checks

Rationale:
- the strict i18n sweep exposed stale labels, generated copy drift, and
  brittle selectors that blocked the frontend validation pipeline
- moving large static data and dialog chunks into focused modules keeps
  the hard compile-time guardrails maintainable instead of relying on
  runtime fallbacks
- fixing the observable UI copy restores deterministic accessible names
  for tests and keeps the locale JSON contract aligned with the UI

Tests:
- corepack pnpm --dir web run format:check
- corepack pnpm --dir web run lint
- PLAYWRIGHT_WEB_PORT=4208 PLAYWRIGHT_PORT=4208 corepack pnpm --dir web exec playwright test --reporter=line --max-failures=1
- ./.codex/skills/push/scripts/openase_ci_gate.sh
